input.user_input: My client is planning to acquire a competitor. How do I assess whether the deal will trigger HSR antitrust filing thresholds?

actual:
To determine whether your client’s proposed acquisition of a competitor triggers a mandatory pre-merger notification under the Hart-Scott-Rodino (HSR) Antitrust Improvements Act, you must conduct a three-part jurisdictional analysis. 

The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) revises the HSR thresholds annually based on changes in the gross national product. The figures below reflect the thresholds that took effect in early 2024. 

Here is the step-by-step framework to assess whether an HSR filing is required:

### 1. The Commerce Test
The first test requires that either the acquiring person or the acquired person is engaged in U.S. commerce or in any activity affecting U.S. commerce. This is a very low bar and is almost always satisfied in modern corporate transactions.

### 2. The Size of Transaction Test
